title: Daily Briefing — Apr 23
headline: Tech ramping, greed rising, but flows say don’t chase just yet.
body: Right, so the rally today is all about tech leadership. SPY at $711.21, QQQ $655.11 — both approaching new highs and snapping above the 20 MA (SPY: 674.96, QQQ: 607.62). Everything looks bullish, VIX is chill at 19.62 and contracting. Fear & Greed screaming up to 68.5, not quite in extreme territory, but the crowd has definitely swung from panic to FOMO in under a month.

Sector rotation? Tech (XLK $158.09) is the clear leader with low risk, while Energy (XLE $56.54) and Materials (XLB $51.83) are weakening despite short-term bounces. Financials (XLF $52.21) are improving, but everything else is lagging hard — Healthcare, Staples, Discretionary all flagged as high risk. Flows still favour mega-cap tech but the rotation is getting thin, so breadth is a concern if leadership narrows.

Contrarian? We're not hitting my AAII or Smart Money extreme levels, but this bounce is classic 'press in the price' — retail piling in after the rally, margin debt ticking up, and most names are miles above their 20MA (AAPL $273.17 vs 20MA $259.45, MSFT $432.92 vs $386.80, ARM +12%). Less trades = more wins: do NOT chase these highs. Abundance mindset — next setup will come when this mania cools. And keep an eye for any sudden HYG/LQD breakdown — if bonds freak out, everything breaks.
callout_body:
SPY: Weekly mean reversion zone at 20MA $674.96 — trend stays up as long as this holds.
XLK: $158.09, breakout above 20MA $142.43 is very strong.
XLE: $56.54, but below 20MA $58.31 — sector rotation weakening.
Arm (ARM): $196.57, +12% — outlier move, but watch for exhaustion.
VIX: 19.62, below 20MA — volatility contraction, don't get lulled.
Fear & Greed: 68.5 — getting hot, but not at sell signal yet.
action_items: Trail stops on any long tech trades (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, ARM) — do not chase new entries at these extended levels.
Watch for sector rotation — if Financials (XLF) reclaim and hold above $52.30 (SMA200), start looking at rotational plays away from lagging Healthcare/Staples.
closing_quote: If it’s in the press, it’s in the price. Patience, react, don’t predict.
